question: List appointments that have no host assigned.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') IS NULL
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no appointments without a host assigned.

question: List completed attended calls from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01.
notes: Past in range and no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: What is the appointment no-show rate?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS no_show_rate_percent
FROM appointments a
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW();

    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id

 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id

    FROM fathom_call_records f_check

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  true AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false

question: List appointments scheduled from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01.
notes: Use schedule_time date range and default list fields.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS no_show_rate_percent_exact
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
ranked_event_types AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    total_past_appointments,
    no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(no_show_rate_percent_exact, 2) AS no_show_rate_percent,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY no_show_rate_percent_exact DESC) AS no_show_rate_rank,
    SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER () AS total_matching_past_appointments
  FROM event_type_no_shows
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  total_past_appointments,
  no_show_appointments,
  no_show_rate_percent,
  total_matching_past_appointments
FROM ranked_event_types
WHERE no_show_rate_rank = 1
ORDER BY total_past_appointments DESC, event_name ASC;

  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id

question: Which appointment outcome is most common?
generated_sql:
WITH outcome_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
    COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
    ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
   AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY
    COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome'),
    COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME')
), max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM outcome_counts
)
SELECT
  oc.outcome_name,
  oc.outcome_role,
  oc.appointment_count,
  oc.total_matching_appointments,
  oc.percentage_of_total
FROM outcome_counts oc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON oc.appointment_count = mc.max_appointment_count
ORDER BY oc.outcome_name ASC;
generated_final_answer: The most common appointment outcome is Won: 128 of 451 appointments (28.38%).

question: List no-show appointments scheduled from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01.
notes: Use a.no_show = true and date range.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Which call category has the most appointments?
generated_sql:
WITH category_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN')
), totals AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(appointment_count) AS total_matching_appointments,
    MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM category_counts
)
SELECT
  cc.call_category,
  cc.appointment_count,
  t.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(cc.appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM category_counts cc
CROSS JOIN totals t
WHERE cc.appointment_count = t.max_appointment_count
ORDER BY cc.call_category ASC;
generated_final_answer: The call category with the most appointments is Sales Call, with 451 appointments (100.00% of all 451 appointments).

question: Which appointment source has the most bookings?
generated_sql:
WITH source_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(CAST(a.source AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS appointment_source,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY COALESCE(CAST(a.source AS text), 'UNKNOWN')
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM source_counts
),
totals AS (
  SELECT SUM(appointment_count) AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM source_counts
)
SELECT
  sc.appointment_source,
  sc.appointment_count,
  t.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(sc.appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM source_counts sc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON sc.appointment_count = mc.max_appointment_count
CROSS JOIN totals t
ORDER BY sc.appointment_source ASC;

question: Which event type has the most appointments?
generated_sql:
WITH event_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
), ranked AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    appointment_count,
    SUM(appointment_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
    ROUND(appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(appointment_count) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total,
    MAX(appointment_count) OVER() AS max_appointment_count
  FROM event_counts
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  appointment_count,
  total_matching_appointments,
  percentage_of_total
FROM ranked
WHERE appointment_count = max_appointment_count
ORDER BY event_name ASC;
generated_final_answer: The event type with the most appointments is **Qualification Call Freedom Academy (TF)**, with **255** of **451** total appointments (**56.54%**).

  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) AS appointments_with_fathom,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT has_fathom_record) AS appointments_without_fathom,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS fathom_coverage_percent,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments
FROM appointment_fathom_flags
GROUP BY host_id
ORDER BY total_past_appointments DESC, host_id ASC;

question: List Fathom calls with objections.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.objections,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.objections IS NOT NULL
  AND f.objections::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}')
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no Fathom calls with objections.

[{"appointment_id": "56f640a7-a01c-4af4-beb3-1b5a46a214ab", "call_started_at": "2026-04-24 09:06:25+00:00", "call_title": "Onboarding Call - Jeffrey Joins Freedom Academy", "display_name": "Jeffrey Nussy", "fathom_record_id": "661e243e-c870-471a-b360-abb15e820689", "schedule_time": "2026-04-24 09:00:00+00:00", "summary": "## Meeting Purpose

[Onboard Jeffrey to the Freedom Academy to achieve financial independence.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Goal:** Achieve financial freedom by liquidating assets (€80k crypto, €150k–€200k home equity) to generate passive income and escape the 9-to-5.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
  - [**Solution:** The Freedom Academy, a lifetime program with a 3–8% monthly return AI bot, a "Be Your Own Bank" system for tax optimization, and a community for support.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [**Decision:** Jeffrey committed to the €5,000 program fee and will liquidate his Audi Q8 (€100k) to fund the bot, aiming for a \~€7k–€8k monthly income.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1948.0)

## Topics

### Jeffrey's Financial Goals & Assets

  - [**Primary Goal:** Achieve financial freedom to spend more time with family, escaping the "hamster wheel" of traditional employment.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)
  - [**Current Assets:**](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=358.0)
      - [**Crypto:** €80k total, with €67k in cash on Bitvavo and €10k–€12k in Bitcoin.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1974.0)
      - [**Real Estate:** €150k–€200k in home equity, which he plans to liquidate by selling his house and renting.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
      - [**Vehicle:** €100k Audi Q8, which he will sell to fund the bot.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=2075.0)
  - [**Long-Term Vision:**](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Build a large enough portfolio to live off passive income.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Own a home in the Netherlands with a small mortgage and potentially a second home abroad (e.g., Spain).](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Provide a strong financial foundation for his children.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### The Freedom Academy Solution

  - [**Program Overview:** A lifetime program providing a complete blueprint for financial independence through crypto.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [**Pillar 1: Foundation & Strategy**](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=806.0)
      - [**Goal:** Build a sustainable, tax-optimized financial system independent of traditional banks.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)
      - [**"Be Your Own Bank" System:** A framework for financial independence using crypto, including non-KYC options and crypto debit cards.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)
      - [**Tax Optimization:** Strategies to legally minimize taxes without physical emigration.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=982.0)
      - [**Portfolio Management:** Guidance on structuring a secure portfolio, including risk diversification and avoiding centralized exchanges like Bitvavo.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=997.0)
  - [**Pillar 2: Passive Income Engine (AI Bot)**](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1031.0)
      - [**Performance:** 3–8% monthly return (30–80% annually) over a 6-year history with no losing months.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1308.0)
      - [**Control:** Capital remains in the user's exchange account; the bot connects via API for automated trading.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)
      - [**Strategy:** The bot uses two modes, switching based on market conditions:](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
          - [**USDT Bot (Bear Market):** Accumulates stablecoins to preserve capital.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
          - [**Bitcoin Bot (Bull Market):** Accumulates Bitcoin for compounded returns (trading profit + asset appreciation).](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
  - [**Pillar 3: Community & Support**](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1168.0)
      - [**Network:** Access to a community of like-minded individuals.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1168.0)
      - [**Support:** 1-on-1 access to 5 community managers for questions.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1168.0)
      - [**Live Calls:** Weekly calls with Didi for exclusive insights and Q\&A.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)
      - [**Expert Speakers:** Sessions with exchange directors and crypto tax advisors.](https://fathom.video/share/CGfmGueqUsxRpzsbYzXRhTzotAqvybML?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)

### Enrollment & Investment

## Topics

### Bot Strategy & Risk Management

  - [**Core Strategy:** Dollar-Cost Averaging (DCA).](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=44.0)
      - [If a trade drops, the bot places new, smaller trades on the downside.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)
      - [This lowers the average entry price, allowing for recovery on a smaller market rebound.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [**Performance Record (6.5 years):**](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=138.0)
      - [No losing months.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=138.0)
      - [No closed trades in a loss.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=138.0)
      - [Typical recovery time for a trade is under two weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=103.0)
  - [**Worst-Case Scenario:** A sudden, massive, one-way market crash (e.g., 90% drop with no bounces).](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=717.0)
      - [**Outcome:** Trades would remain open indefinitely, waiting for a recovery.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This scenario has never occurred and is considered highly improbable.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=717.0)
      - [**Support:** Didi has direct contact with bot developers and exchanges to guide the community in such an event.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=858.0)
  - [**Pair Selection:**](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=162.0)
      - [Developers select pairs (e.g., SOL, XRP, DOGE) for their volatility, which is ideal for the DCA strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/XxqLS1zTShLmcyjZ_dftQvRKDBvq9NWw?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) AS call_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.action_items
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.action_items IS NOT NULL
  AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}')
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: How many Fathom calls from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01 have action items?

  COUNT(*) AS calls_with_action_items
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a

 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.call_started_at >= :start_date
  AND f.call_started_at < :end_date
  AND f.action_items IS NOT NULL
  AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}')

question: What is the average Fathom call duration?
notes: Use linked Fathom records with duration.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS calls_with_duration,
  ROUND(AVG(f.call_duration_seconds) / 60.0, 2) AS avg_call_duration_minutes
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.call_duration_seconds IS NOT NULL;
generated_final_answer: The average Fathom call duration is 43.01 minutes, based on 83 calls with recorded duration.

question: List appointments with Fathom summaries.
notes: Return summary and call context; avoid raw_payload.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(f.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: What was the average Fathom call duration from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS calls_with_duration,
  ROUND(AVG(f.call_duration_seconds) / 60.0, 2) AS avg_call_duration_minutes
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.call_duration_seconds IS NOT NULL
  AND f.call_started_at >= :start_date
  AND f.call_started_at < :end_date;

question: List Fathom call summaries with key points and action items.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''),
    'Untitled Call'
  ) AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.key_points,
  f.action_items
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND (
    NULLIF(TRIM(f.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
    OR (f.key_points IS NOT NULL AND f.key_points::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}'))
    OR (f.action_items IS NOT NULL AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}'))
  )
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which event type has the longest average call duration?
notes: Rank average duration by event type.
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_durations AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS calls_with_duration,
    AVG(f.call_duration_seconds)::numeric AS avg_call_duration_seconds
  FROM fathom_call_records f
  JOIN appointments a
    ON a.id = f.appointment_id
   AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND f.call_duration_seconds IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
max_avg AS (
  SELECT MAX(avg_call_duration_seconds) AS max_avg_call_duration_seconds
  FROM event_type_durations
),
totals AS (
  SELECT SUM(calls_with_duration) AS total_calls_with_duration
  FROM event_type_durations
)
SELECT
  etd.event_name,
  etd.calls_with_duration,
  ROUND(etd.avg_call_duration_seconds / 60.0, 2) AS avg_call_duration_minutes,
  t.total_calls_with_duration
FROM event_type_durations etd
JOIN max_avg m
  ON etd.avg_call_duration_seconds = m.max_avg_call_duration_seconds
CROSS JOIN totals t
ORDER BY etd.calls_with_duration DESC, etd.event_name ASC;
generated_final_answer: Across 83 calls with duration data, Qualification Call Didi Bam Bam has the longest average call duration at 65.33 minutes, based on 1 call.

question: List Fathom calls that have either action items or objections.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) AS call_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  CASE
    WHEN f.action_items IS NOT NULL AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}') THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS has_action_items,
  CASE
    WHEN f.objections IS NOT NULL AND f.objections::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}') THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS has_objections,
  f.action_items,
  f.objections
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND (
    (f.action_items IS NOT NULL AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}'))
    OR
    (f.objections IS NOT NULL AND f.objections::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}'))
  )
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which AI-suggested outcome has the highest average confidence score?
notes: Group by AI outcome and average confidence.
generated_sql:
WITH outcome_confidence AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(ai_outcome.name, 'Unknown AI Suggested Outcome') AS ai_suggested_outcome_name,
    COALESCE(CAST(ai_outcome.role AS text), 'UNKNOWN_AI_OUTCOME') AS ai_suggested_outcome_role,
    COUNT(*) AS fathom_record_count,
    AVG(f.ai_confidence_score::numeric) AS avg_ai_confidence_score_raw,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_fathom_records
  FROM fathom_call_records f
  JOIN appointments a
    ON a.id = f.appointment_id
   AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
  LEFT JOIN sales_statuses ai_outcome
    ON ai_outcome.id = f.ai_suggested_outcome
   AND ai_outcome.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
  WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND f.ai_suggested_outcome IS NOT NULL
    AND f.ai_confidence_score IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    COALESCE(ai_outcome.name, 'Unknown AI Suggested Outcome'),
    COALESCE(CAST(ai_outcome.role AS text), 'UNKNOWN_AI_OUTCOME')
),
max_avg AS (
  SELECT MAX(avg_ai_confidence_score_raw) AS max_avg_ai_confidence_score_raw
  FROM outcome_confidence
)
SELECT
  oc.ai_suggested_outcome_name,
  oc.ai_suggested_outcome_role,
  ROUND(oc.avg_ai_confidence_score_raw, 2) AS avg_ai_confidence_score,
  oc.fathom_record_count,
  oc.total_matching_fathom_records
FROM outcome_confidence oc
JOIN max_avg m
  ON oc.avg_ai_confidence_score_raw = m.max_avg_ai_confidence_score_raw
ORDER BY oc.fathom_record_count DESC, oc.ai_suggested_outcome_name ASC

  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,

  COALESCE(current_outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS current_appointment_outcome_name,
  ROUND(f.ai_confidence_score, 2) AS ai_confidence_score
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id

question: List unmatched Fathom records.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  f.fathom_call_id,
  f.fathom_meeting_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at,
  f.created_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.appointment_id IS NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

WITH months AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 month'
  )::date AS appointment_month
),
monthly_counts AS (
  SELECT
    m.appointment_month,
    COUNT(a.id) AS appointment_count
  FROM months m
  LEFT JOIN appointments a
    ON DATE_TRUNC('month', a.schedule_time)::date = m.appointment_month
   AND a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
   AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
   AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  GROUP BY m.appointment_month
),
monthly_with_previous AS (
  SELECT
    appointment_month,
    appointment_count,
    LAG(appointment_count) OVER (ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS previous_period_appointment_count
  FROM monthly_counts
)
SELECT
  appointment_month,
  appointment_count,
  previous_period_appointment_count,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_appointment_count IS NULL OR previous_period_appointment_count = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((appointment_count - previous_period_appointment_count)::numeric / previous_period_appointment_count) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_previous_period,
  SUM(appointment_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments
FROM monthly_with_previous
ORDER BY appointment_month ASC;

question: Show daily no-show trend from 2026-04-01 to 2026-05-01.
generated_sql:
WITH days AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    CAST(:start_date AS date),
    (CAST(:end_date AS date) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date,
    INTERVAL '1 day'
  )::date AS appointment_date
),
daily_no_shows AS (
  SELECT
    d.appointment_date,
    COUNT(a.id) AS no_show_appointments
  FROM days d
  LEFT JOIN appointments a
    ON a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
   AND a.no_show = true
   AND a.schedule_time >= d.appointment_date
   AND a.schedule_time < d.appointment_date + INTERVAL '1 day'
   AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
   AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
   AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY d.appointment_date
  ORDER BY d.appointment_date ASC
)
SELECT
  SUM(no_show_appointments) AS total_matching_no_show_appointments,
  JSON_AGG(
    JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
      'appointment_date', appointment_date,
      'no_show_appointments', no_show_appointments
    )
    ORDER BY appointment_date ASC
  ) AS daily_no_show_trend
FROM daily_no_shows

FROM weekly_with_previous

question: Show monthly appointment trend by call category from 2025-05-01 to 2026-05-01.
generated_sql:
WITH months AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 month'
  )::date AS appointment_month
),
categories AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS call_category
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
),
monthly_category_counts AS (
  SELECT
    m.appointment_month,
    c.call_category,
    COUNT(a.id) AS appointment_count
  FROM months m
  CROSS JOIN categories c
  LEFT JOIN appointments a
    ON DATE_TRUNC('month', a.schedule_time)::date = m.appointment_month
   AND COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN') = c.call_category
   AND a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
   AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
   AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  GROUP BY m.appointment_month, c.call_category
),
monthly_category_with_previous AS (
  SELECT
    appointment_month,
    call_category,
    appointment_count,
    LAG(appointment_count) OVER (
      PARTITION BY call_category
      ORDER BY appointment_month ASC
    ) AS previous_period_appointment_count
  FROM monthly_category_counts
)
SELECT
  appointment_month,
  call_category,
  appointment_count,
  previous_period_appointment_count,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_appointment_count IS NULL OR previous_period_appointment_count = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((appointment_count - previous_period_appointment_count)::numeric / previous_period_appointment_count) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_previous_period,
  SUM(appointment_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments
FROM monthly_category_with_previous
ORDER BY appointment_month ASC, call_category ASC;

question: Which call category is driving the most booked calls?
generated_sql:
WITH category_counts AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS booked_call_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.snapshot_call_category IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text)
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(booked_call_count) AS max_booked_call_count
  FROM category_counts
)
SELECT
  cc.call_category,
  cc.booked_call_count,
  SUM(cc.booked_call_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(cc.booked_call_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(cc.booked_call_count) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM category_counts cc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON cc.booked_call_count = mc.max_booked_call_count
ORDER BY cc.call_category ASC;

question: Which event type should we review because it has the highest no-show rate?
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_no_show_rates AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    (100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) AS no_show_rate_percent_exact,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
max_rate AS (
  SELECT MAX(no_show_rate_percent_exact) AS max_no_show_rate_percent_exact
  FROM event_type_no_show_rates
)
SELECT
  et.event_name,
  et.total_past_appointments,
  et.no_show_appointments,
  et.no_show_rate_percent,
  et.total_matching_past_appointments
FROM event_type_no_show_rates et
JOIN max_rate mr
  ON et.no_show_rate_percent_exact = mr.max_no_show_rate_percent_exact
ORDER BY et.total_past_appointments DESC, et.event_name ASC;

question: Which host needs the most no-show follow-up?
generated_sql:
WITH host_no_show_stats AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') AS host_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '')
),
ranked_hosts AS (
  SELECT
    host_id,
    total_past_appointments,
    no_show_appointments,
    no_show_rate_percent,
    SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER() AS total_matching_no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * no_show_appointments / NULLIF(SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER(), 0),
      2
    ) AS percentage_of_all_no_shows,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY no_show_appointments DESC) AS no_show_rank
  FROM host_no_show_stats
  WHERE no_show_appointments > 0
)
SELECT
  host_id,
  no_show_appointments,
  total_past_appointments,
  no_show_rate_percent,
  percentage_of_all_no_shows,
  total_matching_no_show_appointments
FROM ranked_hosts
WHERE no_show_rank = 1
ORDER BY host_id ASC;

WITH months AS (

    DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 month'
  )::date AS appointment_month
),
event_types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
),
monthly_counts AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', a.schedule_time)::date AS appointment_month,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY
    DATE_TRUNC('month', a.schedule_time)::date,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
month_event_matrix AS (
  SELECT
    m.appointment_month,
    et.event_name
  FROM months m
  CROSS JOIN event_types et
),
monthly_filled AS (
  SELECT
    mem.appointment_month,
    mem.event_name,
    COALESCE(mc.total_past_appointments, 0) AS total_past_appointments,
    COALESCE(mc.no_show_appointments, 0) AS no_show_appointments,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(mc.total_past_appointments, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE ROUND(100.0 * COALESCE(mc.no_show_appointments, 0) / mc.total_past_appointments, 2)
    END AS no_show_rate_percent
  FROM month_event_matrix mem
  LEFT JOIN monthly_counts mc
    ON mc.appointment_month = mem.appointment_month
   AND mc.event_name = mem.event_name
),
with_previous AS (
  SELECT
    appointment_month,
    event_name,
    total_past_appointments,
    no_show_appointments,
    no_show_rate_percent,
    LAG(total_past_appointments) OVER (PARTITION BY event_name ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS previous_period_total_past_appointments,
    LAG(no_show_appointments) OVER (PARTITION BY event_name ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS previous_period_no_show_appointments,
    LAG(no_show_rate_percent) OVER (PARTITION BY event_name ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS previous_period_no_show_rate_percent,
    SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER (PARTITION BY event_name) AS event_type_total_past_appointments,
    SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER (PARTITION BY event_name) AS event_type_total_no_show_appointments,
    SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER () AS total_matching_past_appointments
  FROM monthly_filled
)
SELECT
  appointment_month,
  event_name,
  total_past_appointments,
  no_show_appointments,
  no_show_rate_percent,
  previous_period_total_past_appointments,
  previous_period_no_show_appointments,
  previous_period_no_show_rate_percent,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_no_show_rate_percent IS NULL OR no_show_rate_percent IS NULL THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(no_show_rate_percent - previous_period_no_show_rate_percent, 2)
  END AS no_show_rate_change_percentage_points,
  event_type_total_past_appointments,
  event_type_total_no_show_appointments,
  total_matching_past_appointments
FROM with_previous
ORDER BY event_type_total_past_appointments DESC, event_name ASC, appointment_month ASC

question: Show weekly appointment trend by event type from 2026-02-09 to 2026-05-04.
generated_sql:
WITH weeks AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('week', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 week'
  )::date AS appointment_week
),
event_types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
),
weekly_event_counts AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('week', a.schedule_time)::date AS appointment_week,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  GROUP BY
    DATE_TRUNC('week', a.schedule_time)::date,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
grid AS (
  SELECT
    w.appointment_week,
    e.event_name
  FROM weeks w
  CROSS JOIN event_types e
),
weekly_with_previous AS (
  SELECT
    g.appointment_week,
    g.event_name,
    COALESCE(wec.appointment_count, 0) AS appointment_count,
    LAG(COALESCE(wec.appointment_count, 0)) OVER (
      PARTITION BY g.event_name
      ORDER BY g.appointment_week ASC
    ) AS previous_period_appointment_count,
    SUM(COALESCE(wec.appointment_count, 0)) OVER (PARTITION BY g.event_name) AS event_type_total_appointments,
    SUM(COALESCE(wec.appointment_count, 0)) OVER () AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM grid g
  LEFT JOIN weekly_event_counts wec
    ON wec.appointment_week = g.appointment_week
   AND wec.event_name = g.event_name
)
SELECT
  appointment_week,
  event_name,
  appointment_count,
  previous_period_appointment_count,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_appointment_count IS NULL OR previous_period_appointment_count = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((appointment_count - previous_period_appointment_count)::numeric / previous_period_appointment_count) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_previous_period,
  event_type_total_appointments,
  total_matching_appointments
FROM weekly_with_previous
ORDER BY appointment_week ASC, event_type_total_appointments DESC, event_name ASC

question: Show weekly no-show trend by host from 2026-02-09 to 2026-05-04.
generated_sql:
WITH weeks AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('week', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 week'
  )::date AS appointment_week
),
hosts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_no_show_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.no_show = true
    AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
    AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host')
),
top_hosts AS (
  SELECT
    host_id,
    total_no_show_appointments,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_no_show_appointments DESC, host_id ASC) AS host_rank
  FROM hosts
),
weekly_host_no_shows AS (
  SELECT
    w.appointment_week,
    th.host_id,
    COUNT(a.id) AS no_show_appointments,
    th.total_no_show_appointments
  FROM weeks w
  JOIN top_hosts th
    ON th.host_rank <= 20
  LEFT JOIN appointments a
    ON DATE_TRUNC('week', a.schedule_time)::date = w.appointment_week
   AND COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') = th.host_id
   AND a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
   AND a.no_show = true
   AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
   AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
   AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY
    w.appointment_week,
    th.host_id,
    th.total_no_show_appointments
),
weekly_with_previous AS (
  SELECT
    appointment_week,
    host_id,
    no_show_appointments,
    LAG(no_show_appointments) OVER (
      PARTITION BY host_id
      ORDER BY appointment_week ASC
    ) AS previous_period_no_show_appointments,
    total_no_show_appointments
  FROM weekly_host_no_shows
)
SELECT
  appointment_week,
  host_id,
  no_show_appointments,
  previous_period_no_show_appointments,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_no_show_appointments IS NULL
      OR previous_period_no_show_appointments = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((no_show_appointments - previous_period_no_show_appointments)::numeric
        / previous_period_no_show_appointments) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_previous_period,
  SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER () AS total_matching_no_show_appointments
FROM weekly_with_previous
ORDER BY appointment_week ASC, total_no_show_appointments DESC, host_id ASC

question: Which setter is booking the highest number of attended calls?
generated_sql:
WITH setter_counts AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') AS setter_id,
    COUNT(*) AS attended_call_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND a.no_show = false
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '')
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(attended_call_count) AS max_attended_call_count
  FROM setter_counts
),
totals AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(SUM(attended_call_count), 0) AS total_matching_attended_calls
  FROM setter_counts
)
SELECT
  sc.setter_id,
  sc.attended_call_count,
  t.total_matching_attended_calls,
  ROUND(sc.attended_call_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_attended_calls, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM setter_counts sc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON sc.attended_call_count = mc.max_attended_call_count
CROSS JOIN totals t
ORDER BY sc.setter_id ASC;

question: Which calls mention objections in the Fathom record?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) AS call_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.objections::text AS objections
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.objections IS NOT NULL
  AND f.objections::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}')
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no calls that mention objections in the Fathom record.

question: Which appointments need operational attention because they are missing Fathom records?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which appointment event types have both high volume and no-shows?
notes: Return appointment count and no-show count by event type.
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_stats AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  total_past_appointments,
  no_show_appointments,
  no_show_rate_percent,
  SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments,
  ROUND(
    total_past_appointments * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER(), 0),
    2
  ) AS percentage_of_total_past_appointments
FROM event_type_stats
WHERE no_show_appointments > 0
ORDER BY no_show_appointments DESC, total_past_appointments DESC, no_show_rate_percent DESC, event_name ASC;

question: What changed in appointment volume from 2026-02-01 to 2026-05-01?
notes: Use monthly trend over explicit previous 3 completed months.
generated_sql:
WITH months AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(:start_date AS timestamp))::date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', (CAST(:end_date AS timestamp) - INTERVAL '1 day'))::date,
    INTERVAL '1 month'
  )::date AS appointment_month
),
monthly_counts AS (
  SELECT
    m.appointment_month,
    COUNT(a.id) AS appointment_count
  FROM months m
  LEFT JOIN appointments a
    ON DATE_TRUNC('month', a.schedule_time)::date = m.appointment_month
   AND a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
   AND a.is_deleted = false
   AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
   AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  GROUP BY m.appointment_month
),
monthly_with_metrics AS (
  SELECT
    appointment_month,
    appointment_count,
    LAG(appointment_count) OVER (ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS previous_period_appointment_count,
    FIRST_VALUE(appointment_count) OVER (ORDER BY appointment_month ASC) AS first_period_appointment_count
  FROM monthly_counts
)
SELECT
  appointment_month,
  appointment_count,
  previous_period_appointment_count,
  CASE
    WHEN previous_period_appointment_count IS NULL OR previous_period_appointment_count = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((appointment_count - previous_period_appointment_count)::numeric / previous_period_appointment_count) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_previous_period,
  first_period_appointment_count,
  appointment_count - first_period_appointment_count AS absolute_change_from_first_period,
  CASE
    WHEN first_period_appointment_count = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      ((appointment_count - first_period_appointment_count)::numeric / first_period_appointment_count) * 100,
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_change_from_first_period,
  SUM(appointment_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments
FROM monthly_with_metrics
ORDER BY appointment_month ASC

question: Which calls have follow-up action items captured by Fathom?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) AS call_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  f.action_items
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.action_items IS NOT NULL
  AND f.action_items::text NOT IN ('null', '[]', '{}', '""')
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT :limit;
